test.name: Document a negative-search result (log_003 MyHeritage nil)
test.description: log_003 records a MyHeritage search for Patrick Flynn in the 1850 Schuylkill County census that returned zero results. The researcher needs to document this nil result as a citable source entry so the negative search is on record. The citation must convey what was searched, what was not found, and where the search was performed.

input.user_message: I need to document the MyHeritage nil result from log_003 as a proper citation so it's on record that I searched there.

judge_context: This is a legitimate citation task — the skill should accept it and produce a negative-search citation, not route away

The citation string must clearly convey it is a negative result, following the SKILL.md negative-search pattern: '[YEAR] [COLLECTION], [JURISDICTION]; searched [SCOPE], no match found; [PLATFORM], accessed [DATE]'
who should name MyHeritage as the platform searched, not as a record creator
where_within should document the search scope: 'Patrick Flynn, born c. 1845, Pennsylvania — no results returned'
The skill should work from log_003's documented query (surname Flynn, given Patrick, birth_year 1845, birth_place Pennsylvania) to populate the citation
Must NOT create a new source entry unless one does not already exist for this search — if creating, it must be linked to log_003
